findings.message: Write tool invoked at line 294 to create temp-agent files but is absent from tools: [Read, Grep, Task] — agent will fail at runtime if tool list is enforced
AskUserQuestion is the agent's primary interaction mechanism (used 15+ times) but is not declared in tools: [Read, Grep, Task]

Absolute path /Users/mbroler/.claude/plugins/repos/orchestration/docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md embeds author's macOS username and points to a file that does not exist anywhere in the repository
Agent does not declare a model field; model tier should be explicit for agents performing multi-step Socratic reasoning
Output format is described inline across process steps rather than in a dedicated ## Output section; makes it hard for callers to parse expected return shape
'comprehensive prompt' uses the vague quantifier 'comprehensive' without defining what completeness criteria make a prompt comprehensive
'Be thorough' is an unquantified instruction; thoroughness criteria are undefined

Absolute path /Users/mbroler/.claude/... embeds author's macOS username; TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md does not exist in the repo; same file referenced portably in creating-workflows/SKILL.md also resolves to nothing
Agent does not declare a model field
'Intuitive' used as a design principle without objective criteria for what makes a name intuitive
'Define behavior clearly' — 'clearly' is unquantified
'comprehensive documentation' — 'comprehensive' is unquantified
commands/create.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/create.md uses Task, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
commands/examples.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/examples.md uses Read, Glob, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
commands/explain.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/explain.md uses Read, AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools
commands/help.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/help.md uses AskUserQuestion but declares no allowed-tools

Quick-reference panel lists /orchestration:run, /orchestration:template, /orchestration:examples, /orchestration:explain without indicating they are deprecated; users following help will invoke deprecated paths
commands/init.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/init.md uses Glob, Read, AskUserQuestion, Write but declares no allowed-tools
commands/menu.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/menu.md uses AskUserQuestion, Glob, Grep but declares no allowed-tools
commands/orchestrate.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/orchestrate.md routes via SlashCommand but declares no allowed-tools
User-supplied workflow name from {{ARGS}} is used as-is in curl -o ./examples/<workflow-name>.flow — a name containing ../ segments could write files outside the examples/ directory
commands/pull.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/pull.md uses Bash (curl) but declares no allowed-tools field
Command requires a workflow identifier argument but has no handler for when {{ARGS}} is empty; the Phase 1 check would silently proceed with an empty string
rm -rf ./temp-scripts/* and rm -f ./temp-agents/*.md executed as cleanup shell commands; limited blast radius and gated by user confirmation, but unconditional glob deletes are worth noting
commands/run.md has no name field in frontmatter; all 10 command files share this pattern suggesting intentional convention, but reduces AI discoverability
commands/run.md uses Task, AskUserQuestion, Bash tools but declares no allowed-tools field in frontmatter
commands/template.md has no name field in frontmatter
commands/template.md uses Read, AskUserQuestion, Glob but declares no allowed-tools
Template command has file-not-found handling but no explicit empty-args handler; user invoking /orchestration:template with no argument gets file search on empty string
'Show relevant example from docs/reference/examples.md' — 'relevant' is unquantified
References docs/TEMP-SCRIPTS-DETECTION-GUIDE.md which is absent from the repository — the link silently fails
'complex multi-agent workflows' — 'complex' is unquantified in the skill trigger description
'I'll proactively create temp scripts' — 'proactively' is vague without a defined detection threshold
'Intuitive — Names/symbols hint at behavior' — no measurable criteria for intuitiveness
'Only when truly needed' — 'truly needed' has no objective threshold

Links to examples/sequential.md, examples/parallel.md, examples/conditional.md, examples/error-handling.md, and examples/checkpoints.md — the skills/executing-workflows/examples/ directory does not exist
'Manage errors gracefully' — 'gracefully' is unquantified
'Add review points for critical steps' — 'critical' is undefined
Five category-specific example files referenced under skills/executing-workflows/examples/ but that directory does not exist in the repository
name: orchestration:executing-workflows uses the plugin namespace prefix; all other skills use bare names (e.g. name: managing-agents) — inconsistent naming convention across the skill set
'Make prompts comprehensive and specific' — both 'comprehensive' and 'specific' are unquantified
'Recommend appropriate tools' — 'appropriate' is unquantified
Skill instructs pip install and npm install patterns with variables sourced from workflow context; no version pinning requirement is shown, and installs run in the user's global environment
'complex multi-step tasks' — 'complex' is unquantified
'Set restrictive file permissions' — 'restrictive' is unquantified
'Show relevant syntax based on user's needs' — 'relevant' is unquantified
